message:
fix(deploy): switch vllm-d console storage to IBM VPC block (#7928) (#7946)

The vllm-d cluster's OpenShift Container Storage (OCS) is degraded:
4 of 6 Ceph OSDs are in Init:CrashLoopBackOff with the encryption-open
init container failing (LUKS unlock). Every PVC mount on the
ocs-storagecluster-cephfs StorageClass hangs with 'MountVolume.MountDevice
failed: rpc error: code = Aborted desc = an operation with the given
Volume ID ... already exists' — the same error other tenants on this
cluster are seeing.

Switch the kc Helm release on vllm-d from ocs-storagecluster-cephfs to
ibmc-vpc-block-10iops-tier (IBM Cloud VPC block). It's RWO (matches the
existing access mode), supports volume expansion, and is completely
independent of the broken Rook-Ceph stack. Also set backup.storageClass
to the same so the db-backup CronJob stops mounting cephfs.

The migration was performed live: cephfs PVCs were unrecoverable (mount
failed on every node we tried), so the data was abandoned and the chart
re-deployed with the new SCs. Init container db-restore would normally
recover the SQLite db from the latest backup file, but the backups PVC
was equally broken — vllm-d's console state starts fresh. Production
state on the pok-prod cluster (separate StorageClass) is unaffected.

This commit pins the new SCs in the workflow so future deploys don't
snap back to cephfs. Once the OCS Ceph OSDs are recovered by an
OpenShift admin, this can optionally be reverted.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
